title: Washing machine drum cleaner
subtitle: A citric acid and bicarbonate wash to descale and deodorise the drum
excerpt: Limescale builds up inside washing machine drums, particularly in hard-water areas. Citric acid dissolves mineral deposits; bicarbonate of soda neutralises odours; tea tree essential oil adds antifungal action on the drum and door seal. Run on a hot empty cycle to clean through.
sourceNotes: Citric acid descaling chemistry from The Complete Book of Clean, Lim (2019). Citric acid and bicarbonate sequence from DIY Natural (diynatural.com, May 2026). Tea tree antifungal use from Hammer et al., Journal of Applied Microbiology (2006).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make up the quantities as needed for each cleaning session. No benefit to making this in advance.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within the session. The citric acid and bicarbonate are separate until used: store each ingredient in its own container and use from the packet each time.

glossaryTerms.definition: Calcium carbonate deposits left behind when hard water evaporates or is heated. Limescale builds up on drum surfaces, heating elements, and pipes, reducing efficiency and causing odours. Acidic cleaning agents: citric acid, white vinegar: dissolve limescale by reacting with the calcium carbonate to form soluble salts.

content.text: Citric acid and bicarbonate of soda neutralise each other when combined in water, so they go into different parts of the machine. The citric acid dissolves

on the drum, heating element, and pipes. The bicarbonate neutralises odours and provides mild abrasion on the drum walls. Run the cycle on the hottest setting available (60 degrees Celsius or above) for the most effective descaling.

After the cycle, wipe the drum, door seal, and door glass with a clean damp cloth. Pull the door seal back and clean the inside of the fold: this is where mould most commonly grows in front-loading machines. Leave the door open for at least 30 minutes after cleaning to air the drum.

items.prepNote: Goes into the main drum (not the dispenser); descales the drum and heating element
Goes into the detergent dispenser; neutralises odours
Approximately 1 ml; add to the bicarbonate in the dispenser; antifungal action on the door seal

content.text: Make sure the drum is empty. Add the citric acid directly to the drum: pour it in loose over the base. Mix the tea tree essential oil into the bicarbonate of soda and pour the mixture into the main detergent compartment of the dispenser drawer. Set the machine to its hottest cycle (60 degrees Celsius or higher) and run empty. Do not add laundry.
After the cycle finishes, wipe down the drum interior, the door seal fold, and the dispenser drawer with a damp cloth. Leave the door ajar to dry. Repeat monthly in hard-water areas.
Keep citric acid and bicarbonate of soda in separate airtight containers. Both keep indefinitely when dry. Do not mix them in advance.
